You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.50 (commit: 2c163c68).

## Issue to Resolve

Change default code style to `ktlint_official`
Starting of ktlint `1.0` the default code style changes from `intellij_idea` (former `kotlin_official`) to `ktlint_official`. The  `ktlint_official` codestyle  adheres to the best parts of  the [Kotlin Coding Conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and [Android Kotlint styleguid](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). But it no longer is restricted to produce code compatible with the default IntelliJ IDEA formatter which  lacks from bug which still are not resolved after multiple years.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `ktlint-rule-engine-core/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/rule/engine/core/api/editorconfig/CodeStyleEditorConfigProperty.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/ClassSignatureRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

1â†’package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig
 2â†’
 3â†’import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
 4â†’import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType.PropertyValueParser.EnumValueParser
 5â†’
 6â†’/**
 7â†’ * Code style to be used while linting and formatting. Note that the [EnumValueParser] requires values to be lowercase.
 8â†’ */
 9â†’@Suppress("EnumEntryName")
10â†’public enum class CodeStyleValue {
11â†’    /**
12â†’     * Code formatting based on Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This
13â†’     * code style aims to be compatible with code formatting in Android Studio.
14â†’     */
15â†’    android_studio,
16â†’
17â†’    /**
18â†’     * Code formatting based on Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). This
19â†’     * code style aims to be compatible with code formatting in IntelliJ IDEA.
20â†’     */
21â†’    intellij_idea,
22â†’
23â†’    /**
24â†’     * Code formatting based on the best of both the Kotlin Coding conventions
25â†’     * (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and Android's Kotlin styleguide
26â†’     * (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This codestyle also provides additional formatting on topics
27â†’     * which are not (explicitly) mentioned in the before mentioned styleguide. Also, this code style sometimes formats
28â†’     * code in a way which is not compatible with the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. When
29â†’     * using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) automatic code formatting in the editor. Mean reason
30â†’     * for deviating from the code formatting provided by the editor is that those contain bugs which after some years
31â†’     * are still not fixed.
32â†’     * In the long run, this code style becomes the default code style provided by KtLint.
33â†’     */
34â†’    ktlint_official,
35â†’}
36â†’
37â†’public val CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY_TYPE: PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType<CodeStyleValue> =
38â†’    PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType(
39â†’        "ktlint_code_style",
40â†’        "The code style ('intellij_idea', 'android_studio' or 'ktlint_official') to be applied. Currently, the " +
41â†’            "value is defaulted to 'intellij_idea' when not set. However, in the future the default code style " +
42â†’            "will be changed to 'ktlint_official'.",
43â†’        SafeEnumValueParser(CodeStyleValue::class.java),
44â†’        CodeStyleValue.values().map { it.name }.toSet(),
45â†’    )
46â†’
47â†’public val CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<CodeStyleValue> =
48â†’    EditorConfigProperty(
49â†’        type = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY_TYPE,
50â†’        /*
51â†’         * Once the [CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official] is matured, it will become the default code style of ktlint. Until
52â†’         * then the [CodeStyleValue.intellij_idea] is used to remain backwards compatible.
53â†’         */
54â†’        defaultValue = CodeStyleValue.intellij_idea,
55â†’        androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue = CodeStyleValue.android_studio,
56â†’        intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue = CodeStyleValue.intellij_idea,
57â†’        ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official,
58â†’    )
59â†’

1â†’package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
  2â†’

  5â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.CLASS

  8â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.COMMA
  9â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.MODIFIER_LIST

 21â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule.VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule.Mode.ONLY_WHEN_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_AND_ENABLED
 22â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule.VisitorModifier.RunAsLateAsPossible
 23â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
 24â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.children
 25â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY
 26â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official
 27â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfig

 29â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY
 30â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY
 31â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY

 33â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.indent
 34â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isPartOfComment
 35â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpace
 36â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline
 37â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextCodeLeaf
 38â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextCodeSibling
 39â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextLeaf
 40â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevLeaf
 41â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevSibling
 42â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe
 43â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe
 44â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 45â†’import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
 46â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 47â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafElement
 48â†’
 49â†’/**
 50â†’ * Formats the class signature according to https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#class-headers
 51â†’ *
 52â†’ * In code style `ktlint_official` class headers containing 2 or more parameters are formatted as multiline signature. As the Kotlin Coding
 53â†’ * conventions do not specify what is meant with a "few parameters", no default is set for other code styles.
 54â†’ */
 55â†’public class ClassSignatureRule :
 56â†’    StandardRule(
 57â†’        id = "class-signature",
 58â†’        visitorModifiers =
 59â†’            setOf(
 60â†’                // Run after wrapping and spacing rules
 61â†’                RunAsLateAsPossible,
 62â†’                RunAfterRule(DISCOURAGED_COMMENT_LOCATION_RULE_ID, ONLY_WHEN_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_AND_ENABLED),
 63â†’            ),
 64â†’        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 65â†’            setOf(
 66â†’                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 67â†’                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 68â†’                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 69â†’                FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY,
 70â†’            ),
 71â†’    ),
 72â†’    Rule.Experimental {
 73â†’    private var codeStyle = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 74â†’    private var indentConfig = DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 75â†’    private var maxLineLength = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 76â†’    private var classSignatureWrappingMinimumParameters = FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 77â†’
 78â†’    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 79â†’        codeStyle = editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
 80â†’        classSignatureWrappingMinimumParameters = editorConfig[FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY]
 81â†’        indentConfig =
 82â†’            IndentConfig(
 83â†’                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 84â†’                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 85â†’            )
 86â†’        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 87â†’    }
 88â†’
 89â†’    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 90â†’        node: ASTNode,
 91â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 92â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 93â†’    ) {
 94â†’        if (node.elementType == CLASS) {
 95â†’            visitClass(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 96â†’        }
 97â†’    }
 98â†’
 99â†’    private fun ASTNode.classSignatureNodes(excludeSuperTypes: Boolean): List<ASTNode> {
100â†’        // Find the nodes that are to be placed on the same line if no max line length is set
101â†’        //     internal class Foo(bar: String) { // Class without super type
102â†’        // or
103â†’        //     public class Foo(bar: String) : Bar { // Class with exactly one super type
104â†’        // or
105â†’        //     private class Foo(bar: String) : // Class with multiple super types which have to be wrapped to next lines
106â†’        val firstCodeChild = getFirstChildInSignature()
107â†’        val lastNodeInPrimaryClassSignatureLine =
108â†’            takeIf { excludeSuperTypes }
109â†’                // When the class extends multiple super types or if the super type list contains a newline, all super types are wrapped
110â†’                // on separate line
111â†’                ?.findChildByType(COLON)
112â†’                ?: findChildByType(CLASS_BODY)?.firstChildNode
113â†’        return collectLeavesRecursively()
114â†’            .childrenBetween(
115â†’                startASTNodePredicate = { it == firstCodeChild },
116â†’                endASTNodePredicate = { it == lastNodeInPrimaryClassSignatureLine },
117â†’            )
118â†’    }
119â†’
120â†’    private fun ASTNode.superTypes() =
121â†’        findChildByType(SUPER_TYPE_LIST)
122â†’            ?.children()
123â†’            ?.filterNot { it.isWhiteSpace() || it.isPartOfComment() || it.elementType == COMMA }
124â†’
125â†’    private fun ASTNode.hasMultilineSuperTypeList() = findChildByType(SUPER_TYPE_LIST)?.textContains('\n') == true
126â†’
127â†’    private fun ASTNode.getFirstChildInSignature(): ASTNode? {
128â†’        findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
129â†’            ?.let { modifierList ->
130â†’                val iterator = modifierList.children().iterator()
131â†’                var currentNode: ASTNode
132â†’                while (iterator.hasNext()) {
133â†’                    currentNode = iterator.next()
134â†’                    if (currentNode.elementType != ANNOTATION &&
135â†’                        currentNode.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY &&
136â†’                        currentNode.elementType != WHITE_SPACE
137â†’                    ) {
138â†’                        return currentNode
139â†’                    }
140â†’                }
141â†’                return modifierList.nextCodeSibling()
142â†’            }
143â†’        return nextCodeLeaf()
144â†’    }
145â†’
146â†’    private fun visitClass(
147â†’        node: ASTNode,
148â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
149â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
150â†’    ) {
151â†’        require(node.elementType == CLASS)
152â†’
153â†’        val wrapPrimaryConstructorParameters =
154â†’            node.hasMinimumNumberOfParameters() ||
155â†’                node.containsMultilineParameter() ||
156â†’                (codeStyle == ktlint_official && node.containsAnnotatedParameter()) ||
157â†’                (isMaxLineLengthSet() && classSignatureExcludingSuperTypesExceedsMaxLineLength(node, emit, autoCorrect)) ||
158â†’                (!isMaxLineLengthSet() && node.classSignatureExcludingSuperTypesIsMultiline())
159â†’        fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = wrapPrimaryConstructorParameters, dryRun = false)
160â†’        fixWhitespacesInSuperTypeList(node, emit, autoCorrect, wrappedPrimaryConstructor = wrapPrimaryConstructorParameters)
161â†’        fixClassBody(node, emit, autoCorrect)
162â†’    }
163â†’
164â†’    private fun classSignatureExcludingSuperTypesExceedsMaxLineLength(
165â†’        node: ASTNode,
166â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
167â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
168â†’    ): Boolean {
169â†’        val actualClassSignatureLength = node.getClassSignatureLength(excludeSuperTypes = true)
170â†’        // Calculate the length of the class signature in case it, excluding the super types, would be rewritten as single
171â†’        // line (and without a maximum line length). The white space correction will be calculated via a dry run of the
172â†’        // actual fix.
173â†’        val length =
174â†’            actualClassSignatureLength +
175â†’                // Calculate the white space correction in case the signature would be rewritten to a single line
176â†’                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = false, dryRun = true)
177â†’        return length > maxLineLength
178â†’    }
179â†’
180â†’    private fun ASTNode.classSignatureExcludingSuperTypesIsMultiline() =
181â†’        classSignatureNodes(excludeSuperTypes = true)
182â†’            .any { it.textContains('\n') }
183â†’
184â†’    private fun ASTNode.getClassSignatureLength(excludeSuperTypes: Boolean) =
185â†’        indent(false).length + getClassSignatureNodesLength(excludeSuperTypes)
186â†’
187â†’    private fun ASTNode.getClassSignatureNodesLength(excludeSuperTypes: Boolean) =
188â†’        classSignatureNodes(excludeSuperTypes)
189â†’            .joinTextToString()
190â†’            .length
191â†’
192â†’    private fun ASTNode.containsMultilineParameter(): Boolean =
193â†’        getPrimaryConstructorParameterListOrNull()
194â†’            ?.children()
195â†’            .orEmpty()
196â†’            .filter { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
197â†’            .any { it.textContains('\n') }
198â†’
199â†’    private fun ASTNode.containsAnnotatedParameter(): Boolean =
200â†’        getPrimaryConstructorParameterListOrNull()
201â†’            ?.children()
202â†’            .orEmpty()
203â†’            .filter { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
204â†’            .any { it.isAnnotated() }
205â†’
206â†’    private fun ASTNode.isAnnotated() =
207â†’        findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
208â†’            ?.children()
209â†’            .orEmpty()
210â†’            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
211â†’
212â†’    private fun fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(
213â†’        node: ASTNode,
214â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
215â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
216â†’        multiline: Boolean,
217â†’        dryRun: Boolean,
218â†’    ): Int {
219â†’        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
220â†’
221â†’        val primaryConstructorParameterList = node.getPrimaryConstructorParameterListOrNull()
222â†’        val hasNoValueParameters =
223â†’            primaryConstructorParameterList
224â†’                ?.children()
225â†’                .orEmpty()
226â†’                .none { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
227â†’
228â†’        whiteSpaceCorrection +=
229â†’            if (hasNoValueParameters) {
230â†’                fixWhiteSpacesInEmptyValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, dryRun)
231â†’            } else {
232â†’                fixWhiteSpacesBeforeFirstParameterInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline, dryRun) +
233â†’                    fixWhiteSpacesBetweenParametersInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline, dryRun) +
234â†’                    fixWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline, dryRun)
235â†’            }
236â†’
237â†’        return whiteSpaceCorrection
238â†’    }
239â†’
240â†’    private fun fixWhiteSpacesInEmptyValueParameterList(
241â†’        node: ASTNode,
242â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
243â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
244â†’        dryRun: Boolean,
245â†’    ): Int {
246â†’        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
247â†’
248â†’        node
249â†’            .getPrimaryConstructorParameterListOrNull()
250â†’            ?.takeUnless { it.containsComment() }
251â†’            ?.let { parameterList ->
252â†’                if (!dryRun) {
253â†’                    emit(parameterList.startOffset, "No parenthesis expected", true)
254â†’                }
255â†’                if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
256â†’                    parameterList.treeParent.removeChild(parameterList)
257â†’                } else {
258â†’                    whiteSpaceCorrection -= parameterList.textLength
259â†’                }
260â†’            }
261â†’
262â†’        return whiteSpaceCorrection
263â†’    }
264â†’
265â†’    private fun ASTNode.containsComment() = children().any { it.isPartOfComment() }
266â†’
267â†’    private fun fixWhiteSpacesBeforeFirstParameterInValueParameterList(
268â†’        node: ASTNode,
269â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
270â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
271â†’        multiline: Boolean,
272â†’        dryRun: Boolean,
273â†’    ): Int {
274â†’        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
275â†’
276â†’        val valueParameterList = node.getPrimaryConstructorParameterListOrNull()
277â†’        val firstParameterInList =
278â†’            valueParameterList
279â†’                ?.children()
280â†’                ?.first { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
281â†’                ?: return 0
282â†’
283â†’        val firstParameter = firstParameterInList.firstChildNode
284â†’        firstParameter
285â†’            ?.prevLeaf()
286â†’            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
287â†’            .let { whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier ->
288â†’                if (multiline) {
289â†’                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier == null ||
290â†’                        !whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.textContains('\n')
291â†’                    ) {
292â†’                        if (!dryRun) {
293â†’                            emit(firstParameterInList.startOffset, "Newline expected after opening parenthesis", true)
294â†’                        }
295â†’                        // Let indent rule determine the exact indent
296â†’                        val expectedParameterIndent = indentConfig.childIndentOf(node)
297â†’                        if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
298â†’                            valueParameterList.firstChildNode.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(expectedParameterIndent)
299â†’                        } else {
300â†’                            whiteSpaceCorrection += expectedParameterIndent.length - (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier?.textLength ?: 0)
301â†’                        }
302â†’                    }
303â†’                } else {
304â†’                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier != null) {
305â†’                        if (!dryRun) {
306â†’                            emit(
307â†’                                firstParameter!!.startOffset,
308â†’                                "No whitespace expected between opening parenthesis and first parameter name",
309â†’                                true,
310â†’                            )
311â†’                        }
312â†’                        if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
313â†’                            whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.treeParent.removeChild(whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier)
314â†’                        } else {
315â†’                            whiteSpaceCorrection -= whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.textLength
316â†’                        }
317â†’                    }
318â†’                }
319â†’            }
320â†’
321â†’        return whiteSpaceCorrection
322â†’    }
323â†’
324â†’    private fun fixWhiteSpacesBetweenParametersInValueParameterList(
325â†’        node: ASTNode,
326â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
327â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
328â†’        multiline: Boolean,
329â†’        dryRun: Boolean,
330â†’    ): Int {
331â†’        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
332â†’
333â†’        val valueParameterList = node.getPrimaryConstructorParameterListOrNull()
334â†’        val firstParameterInList =
335â†’            valueParameterList
336â†’                ?.children()
337â†’                ?.first { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
338â†’                ?: return 0
339â†’
340â†’        valueParameterList
341â†’            .children()
342â†’            .filter { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }

1â†’package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig
  2â†’
  3â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.logger.api.initKtLintKLogger
  4â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
  5â†’import mu.KotlinLogging
  6â†’import org.ec4j.core.model.Property
  7â†’import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
  8â†’import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType.PropertyValue
  9â†’
 10â†’private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
 11â†’
 12â†’/**
 13â†’ * Loaded [Property]s from `.editorconfig` files.
 14â†’ */
 15â†’public data class EditorConfig(
 16â†’    private val properties: Map<String, Property> = emptyMap(),
 17â†’) {
 18â†’    public constructor(vararg properties: Property) : this(
 19â†’        properties.associateBy { property -> property.name },
 20â†’    )
 21â†’
 22â†’    private val codeStyle: CodeStyleValue
 23â†’        get() =
 24â†’            with(CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY) {
 25â†’                // Prevent printing a lot of warnings when the code_style_value is not defined explicitly by not using the function to get
 26â†’                // the default value for the property
 27â†’                type
 28â†’                    .getPropertyValue(type.name)
 29â†’                    .parsed
 30â†’                    ?: defaultValue
 31â†’            }
 32â†’
 33â†’    /**
 34â†’     * Gets the value of [editorConfigProperty] from [EditorConfig] provided that the name of the property is identical to the name of the
 35â†’     * type of the property. If the value is not found, the default value for the matching code style is returned.
 36â†’     */
 37â†’    public operator fun <T> get(editorConfigProperty: EditorConfigProperty<T>): T {
 38â†’        when {
 39â†’            editorConfigProperty.deprecationError != null ->
 40â†’                throw DeprecatedEditorConfigPropertyException(
 41â†’                    "Property '${editorConfigProperty.name}' is disallowed: ${editorConfigProperty.deprecationError}",
 42â†’                )
 43â†’
 44â†’            editorConfigProperty.deprecationWarning != null ->
 45â†’                LOGGER.warn { "Property '${editorConfigProperty.name}' is deprecated: ${editorConfigProperty.deprecationWarning}" }
 46â†’        }
 47â†’        val property =
 48â†’            properties.getOrElse(editorConfigProperty.name) {
 49â†’                throw IllegalStateException(
 50â†’                    "Property '${editorConfigProperty.name}' can not be retrieved from this EditorConfig. Note that the EditorConfig " +
 51â†’                        "which is provided to class '${Rule::class.qualifiedName}' only contains the properties which are defined in the " +
 52â†’                        "property '${Rule::usesEditorConfigProperties.name}'.",
 53â†’                )
 54â†’            }
 55â†’
 56â†’        editorConfigProperty
 57â†’            .propertyMapper
 58â†’            ?.invoke(property, codeStyle)
 59â†’            ?.let { newValue ->
 60â†’                // If the property value is remapped to a non-null value then return it immediately.
 61â†’                val originalValue = property.sourceValue
 62â†’                if (newValue.toString() != originalValue) {
 63â†’                    LOGGER.trace {
 64â†’                        "Value of '.editorconfig' property '${editorConfigProperty.name}' is remapped " +
 65â†’                            "from '$originalValue' to '$newValue'"
 66â†’                    }
 67â†’                }
 68â†’                return newValue
 69â†’            }
 70â†’
 71â†’        return if (property.isUnset) {
 72â†’            editorConfigProperty
 73â†’                .getDefaultValue()
 74â†’                .also {
 75â†’                    LOGGER.trace {
 76â†’                        "No value of '.editorconfig' property '${editorConfigProperty.name}' was found. Value has been defaulted to " +
 77â†’                            "'$it'. Setting the value explicitly in '.editorconfig' removes this message from the log."
 78â†’                    }
 79â†’                }
 80â†’        } else {
 81â†’            // In case the EditorConfig is loaded incorrectly it can happen that the type field of the loaded property is null. Getting the
 82â†’            // value via "property.getValueAs()" can lead to a class cast exception as the actual type of the value returned is transformed
 83â†’            // to a String instead of type T. Getting the value via the editorConfigProperty type is safe as it can not be null.
 84â†’            editorConfigProperty.getParsedValueOrDefault() ?: editorConfigProperty.getDefaultValue()
 85â†’        }
 86â†’    }
 87â†’
 88â†’    /**
 89â†’     * Gets the value of the property with [propertyType] and name [propertyName] from [EditorConfig]. Returns null if the type is not
 90â†’     * found. Also, returns null when no property with the name is found.
 91â†’     */
 92â†’    public fun <T> getEditorConfigValueOrNull(
 93â†’        propertyType: PropertyType<T>,
 94â†’        propertyName: String,
 95â†’    ): T? = propertyType.getPropertyValue(propertyName).parsed
 96â†’
 97â†’    private fun <T> EditorConfigProperty<T>.getDefaultValue() =
 98â†’        when (codeStyle) {
 99â†’            CodeStyleValue.android_studio -> androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue
100â†’            CodeStyleValue.intellij_idea -> intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue
101â†’            CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official -> ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue
102â†’        }
103â†’
104â†’    /**
105â†’     * Checks whether a property with name [propertyName] is defined.
106â†’     */
107â†’    public fun contains(propertyName: String): Boolean = properties.contains(propertyName)
108â†’
109â†’    /**
110â†’     * Gets the parsed value of the property with type [PropertyType]. Returns null if the property type is not found. Can only be used for
111â†’     * properties for which the name of the property is identical to the name of the type.
112â†’     */
113â†’    private fun <T> EditorConfigProperty<T>.getParsedValueOrDefault(): T? =
114â†’        this.type.getParsedValueOrDefault(this.type.name, this.defaultValue)
115â†’
116â†’    /**
117â†’     * Gets the parsed value of the property with type [PropertyType] and name [propertyName]. Returns null if the property type is not
118â†’     * found. Intended to be used for properties for which the name is not identical to the name of the property type.
119â†’     */
120â†’    private fun <T> PropertyType<T>.getParsedValueOrDefault(
121â†’        propertyName: String,
122â†’        defaultValue: T,
123â†’    ): T =
124â†’        getPropertyValue(propertyName)
125â†’            .let { propertyValue ->
126â†’                if (propertyValue.isValid) {
127â†’                    propertyValue.parsed
128â†’                } else {
129â†’                    LOGGER.warn {
130â†’                        "Editorconfig property '$propertyName' contains an invalid value '${propertyValue.source}'. The default value " +
131â†’                            "'$defaultValue' is used instead."
132â†’                    }
133â†’                    defaultValue
134â†’                }
135â†’            }
136â†’
137â†’    private fun <T> PropertyType<T>.getPropertyValue(propertyName: String): PropertyValue<T> =
138â†’        parse(
139â†’            properties[propertyName]?.sourceValue,
140â†’        )
141â†’
142â†’    /**
143â†’     * Maps all properties with given [mapper] to a collection.
144â†’     */
145â†’    public fun <T> map(mapper: (Property) -> T): Collection<T> =
146â†’        properties
147â†’            .values
148â†’            .map { mapper(it) }
149â†’
150â†’    /**
151â†’     * Adds the given [EditorConfigProperty]'s with its default value for the active code style to the [EditorConfig] if the property does
152â†’     * not yet exist.
153â†’     */
154â†’    public fun addPropertiesWithDefaultValueIfMissing(vararg defaultEditorConfigProperties: EditorConfigProperty<*>): EditorConfig {
155â†’        val editorConfigProperties = defaultEditorConfigProperties.toSet()
156â†’        requireSingularIdentities(editorConfigProperties)
157â†’        return editorConfigProperties
158â†’            .defaultProperties()
159â†’            .plus(
160â†’                // Overwrite default properties with values that were actually already defined in the EditorConfig
161â†’                properties,
162â†’            ).let { EditorConfig(it) }
163â†’    }
164â†’
165â†’    /**
166â†’     * Creates an [EditorConfig] containing [editorConfigProperties] only. Retains the value of the property when defined in the original
167â†’     * [EditorConfig]. If the property does not exist in the original [EditorConfig], it will be added and its value is set to the default
168â†’     * value of the active code style.
169â†’     */
170â†’    public fun filterBy(editorConfigProperties: Set<EditorConfigProperty<*>>): EditorConfig {
171â†’        requireSingularIdentities(editorConfigProperties)
172â†’        return editorConfigProperties
173â†’            .defaultProperties()
174â†’            .plus(
175â†’                // Overwrite default properties with values that were actually already defined in the EditorConfig
176â†’                properties.filterBy(editorConfigProperties.map { it.name }),
177â†’            ).let { EditorConfig(it) }
178â†’    }
179â†’
180â†’    private fun requireSingularIdentities(editorConfigProperties: Set<EditorConfigProperty<*>>) {
181â†’        val editorConfigPropertiesWithMultipleIdentities =
182â†’            editorConfigProperties
183â†’                .groupBy { it.name }
184â†’                .filterValues { groupedEditorConfigProperties ->
185â†’                    groupedEditorConfigProperties
186â†’                        .distinctBy { it.hashCode() }
187â†’                        .count() > 1
188â†’                }
189â†’        require(editorConfigPropertiesWithMultipleIdentities.isEmpty()) {
190â†’            editorConfigPropertiesWithMultipleIdentities
191â†’                .map { (name, editorConfigProperties) ->
192â†’                    editorConfigProperties
193â†’                        .joinToString(
194â†’                            separator = "\n",
195â†’                            prefix = "Found multiple editorconfig properties with name '$name' but having distinct identities:\n",
196â†’                        ) { "  - $it" }
197â†’                }.joinToString(separator = "\n")
198â†’        }
199â†’    }
200â†’
201â†’    private fun Set<EditorConfigProperty<*>>.defaultProperties(): Map<String, Property> {
202â†’        return associate { editorConfigProperty ->
203â†’            editorConfigProperty
204â†’                .writeDefaultValue()
205â†’                .let { editorConfigProperty.name to editorConfigProperty.toPropertyWithValue(it) }
206â†’        }
207â†’    }
208â†’
209â†’    private fun <T> EditorConfigProperty<T>.writeDefaultValue() = propertyWriter(getDefaultValue())
210â†’
211â†’    private fun <T> EditorConfigProperty<T>.toPropertyWithValue(value: String): Property =
212â†’        Property
213â†’            .builder()
214â†’            .type(type)
215â†’            .name(name)
216â†’            .value(value)
217â†’            .build()
218â†’
219â†’    private fun Map<String, Property>.filterBy(editorConfigProperties: List<String>): Map<String, Property> =
220â†’        filterKeys { it in editorConfigProperties }
221â†’}
222â†’
223â†’private class DeprecatedEditorConfigPropertyException(
224â†’    message: String,
225â†’) : RuntimeException(message)
226â†’

1â†’package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig
 2â†’
 3â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.logger.api.initKtLintKLogger
 4â†’import mu.KotlinLogging
 5â†’import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
 6â†’
 7â†’private const val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_ANDROID_STUDIO_CODE_STYLE = 100 // https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide#line_wrapping
 8â†’private const val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_KTLINT_OFFICIAL_CODE_STYLE = 140
 9â†’private const val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF_EDITOR_CONFIG = "off"
10â†’
11â†’/**
12â†’ * Integer value that denotes that the property is to be considered as disabled.
13â†’ */
14â†’public const val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF: Int = Int.MAX_VALUE
15â†’
16â†’private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
17â†’private var isInvalidValueLoggedBefore = false
18â†’
19â†’public val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<Int> =
20â†’    EditorConfigProperty(
21â†’        name = PropertyType.max_line_length.name,
22â†’        type = PropertyType.max_line_length,
23â†’        defaultValue = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF,
24â†’        androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_ANDROID_STUDIO_CODE_STYLE,
25â†’        intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF,
26â†’        ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_KTLINT_OFFICIAL_CODE_STYLE,
27â†’        propertyMapper = { property, codeStyleValue ->
28â†’            when {
29â†’                property == null || property.isUnset -> {
30â†’                    codeStyleValue.defaultValue()
31â†’                }
32â†’
33â†’                /**
34â†’                 * Internally, Ktlint uses integer 'Int.MAX_VALUE' to indicate that the max line length has to be ignored as this is easier
35â†’                 * in comparisons to check whether the maximum length of a line is exceeded.
36â†’                 */
37â†’                property.sourceValue == MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF_EDITOR_CONFIG -> MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF
38â†’
39â†’                else ->
40â†’                    PropertyType
41â†’                        .max_line_length
42â†’                        .parse(property.sourceValue)
43â†’                        .let {
44â†’                            if (!it.isValid) {
45â†’                                if (!isInvalidValueLoggedBefore) {
46â†’                                    isInvalidValueLoggedBefore = true
47â†’                                    LOGGER.warn { "Found invalid '.editorconfig' property value: ${it.errorMessage}" }
48â†’                                }
49â†’                                if (it.source == "-1") {
50â†’                                    MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF
51â†’                                } else {
52â†’                                    codeStyleValue.defaultValue()
53â†’                                }
54â†’                            } else {
55â†’                                it.parsed
56â†’                            }
57â†’                        }
58â†’            }
59â†’        },
60â†’        propertyWriter = { property ->
61â†’            if (property <= 0 || property == MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF) {
62â†’                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF_EDITOR_CONFIG
63â†’            } else {

65â†’            }
66â†’        },
67â†’    )
68â†’
69â†’private fun CodeStyleValue.defaultValue() =
70â†’    when (this) {

1â†’package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.internal
 2â†’
 3â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.EditorConfigOverride
 4â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
 5â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY
 6â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.CodeStyleValue
 7â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfig
 8â†’import java.nio.file.FileSystem
 9â†’import java.nio.file.FileSystems
10â†’import java.nio.file.Path
11â†’
12â†’/**
13â†’ * Generates a Kotlin section content for the `.editorconfig` file. The set of properties is based on the properties used by the given set
14â†’ * of [Rule]s. The default values for missing properties are based on the given [CodeStyleValue].
15â†’ */
16â†’internal class EditorConfigGenerator(
17â†’    private val fileSystem: FileSystem = FileSystems.getDefault(),
18â†’    private val editorConfigLoaderEc4j: EditorConfigLoaderEc4j,
19â†’) {
20â†’    /**
21â†’     * Method loads merged `.editorconfig` content using [filePath], and then, by querying the set of [Rule]s generates Kotlin section
22â†’     * (default is `[*.{kt,kts}]`) content including expected default values.
23â†’     *
24â†’     * @return Kotlin section editorconfig content. For example:
25â†’     * ```properties
26â†’     * final-newline = true
27â†’     * indent-size = 4
28â†’     * ```
29â†’     */
30â†’    fun generateEditorconfig(
31â†’        rules: Set<Rule>,
32â†’        codeStyle: CodeStyleValue,
33â†’        filePath: Path,
34â†’    ): String =
35â†’        editorConfig(rules, codeStyle, filePath)
36â†’            .map { "${it.name} = ${it.sourceValue}" }
37â†’            .distinct()
38â†’            .sorted()
39â†’            .joinToString(separator = System.lineSeparator())
40â†’
41â†’    private fun editorConfig(
42â†’        rules: Set<Rule>,
43â†’        codeStyle: CodeStyleValue,
44â†’        filePath: Path,
45â†’    ): EditorConfig {
46â†’        val usedEditorConfigProperties =
47â†’            rules
48â†’                .map { it.usesEditorConfigProperties }
49â†’                .flatten()
50â†’                .toSet()
51â†’        return loadEditorConfig(codeStyle, filePath)
52â†’            .filterBy(usedEditorConfigProperties.plus(DEFAULT_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTIES))
53â†’    }
54â†’
55â†’    private fun loadEditorConfig(
56â†’        codeStyle: CodeStyleValue,
57â†’        filePath: Path,
58â†’    ) = EditorConfigLoader(
59â†’        fileSystem = fileSystem,
60â†’        editorConfigLoaderEc4j = editorConfigLoaderEc4j,
61â†’        editorConfigOverride = EditorConfigOverride.from(CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY to codeStyle.name),
62â†’    ).load(filePath = filePath)
63â†’}
64â†’

CHANGELOG.md:9:* Add rule `class-signature`. This rule rewrites the class header to a consistent format. In code style `ktlint_official`, super types are always wrapped to a separate line. In other code styles, super types are only wrapped in classes having multiple super types. Especially for code style `ktlint_official` the class headers are rewritten in a more consistent format. See [examples in documentation](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/latest/rules/experimental/#class-signature). `class-signature` [#875](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1349), [#1349](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/875)
--
CHANGELOG.md:17:* Do not indent class body for classes having a long super type list in code style `ktlint_official` as it is inconsistent compared to other class bodies `indent` [#2115](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2115)
CHANGELOG.md:18:* In code style `ktlint_official` do not indent the closing parenthesis of a PARENTHESIZED expression `indent` [#920](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/920)
--
CHANGELOG.md:67:* Add new experimental rule `no-empty-file` for all code styles. A kotlin (script) file may not be empty ([#1074](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1074))
--
CHANGELOG.md:108:* Allow to 'unset' the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_function_signature_rule_force_multiline_when_parameter_count_greater_or_equal_than` when using `ktlint_official` code style `function-signature` ([#1977](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1977))
--
CHANGELOG.md:111:* Restrict indentation of closing quotes to `ktlint_official` code style to keep formatting of other code styles consistent with `0.48.x` and before `indent` ([#1971](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1971))
CHANGELOG.md:112:* Extract rule `no-single-line-block-comment` from `comment-wrapping` rule. The `no-single-line-block-comment` rule is added as experimental rule to the `ktlint_official` code style, but it can be enabled explicitly for the other code styles as well. ([#1980](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1980))
--
CHANGELOG.md:174:Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. New fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
--
CHANGELOG.md:178:#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
--
CHANGELOG.md:180:A new code style `ktlint_official` is introduced. This code style is work in progress but will become the default code style in the `1.0` release. Please try out the new code style and provide your feedback via the [issue tracker](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues).
--
CHANGELOG.md:182:This `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This code style also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in those conventions and style guide. But do note that this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor.
--
CHANGELOG.md:184:The existing code styles have been renamed to make more clear what the basis of the code style is.
--
CHANGELOG.md:186:The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint `0.49`. Note that the default code style will be changed to `ktlint_official` in the `1.0` release.
--
CHANGELOG.md:188:Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
--
CHANGELOG.md:381:* Add new code style `ktlint_offical`. The code style is work in progress and should be considered a preview. It is intended to become the default code style in the next release. Please try it out and give your feedback. See [code styles](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/code-styles/) for more information. The following rules have been added to the `ktlint_official` code style (the rules can also be run for other code styles when enabled explicitly):
--
CHANGELOG.md:385:  * Add new experimental rule `try-catch-finally-spacing`. This rule enforces consistent spacing in try-catch, try-finally and try-catch-finally statement. This rule can also be run for other code styles, but then it needs to be enabled explicitly.
--
CHANGELOG.md:403:* Add new experimental rule `enum-wrapping` for all code styles. An enum should either be a single line, or each enum entry should be defined on a separate line. ([#1903](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1903))
--
CHANGELOG.md:416:* The parameters of a function literal containing a multiline parameter list are aligned with first parameter whenever the first parameter is on the same line as the start of that function literal (not allowed in `ktlint_official` code style) `indent` ([#1756](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1756))
--
CHANGELOG.md:418:* Fix continuation indent for a dot qualified array access expression in `ktlint_official` code style only `indent` ([#1540](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1540))
--
CHANGELOG.md:436:* Wrap annotated function parameters to a separate line in code style `ktlint_official` only. `function-signature`, `parameter-list-wrapping` ([#1908](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1908))
--
CHANGELOG.md:439:* Wrap annotations before class constructor in code style `ktlint_official`. `annotation` ([#1916](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1916))
--
CHANGELOG.md:444:* Wrap the parameters of a function literal containing a multiline parameter list (only in `ktlint_official` code style) `parameter-list-wrapping` ([#1681](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1681)).
--
CHANGELOG.md:453:* For the new code style `ktlint_official`, do not allow wildcard imports `java.util` and `kotlinx.android.synthetic` by default. Important: `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand` needs to be set to value `unset` in order to enforce IntelliJ IDEA default formatter to not generate wildcard imports `no-wildcard-imports` ([#1797](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1797))
--
CHANGELOG.md:932:This property defaults to the `official` Kotlin code style when not set.

conversation_history.content:
1â†’Ktlint uses a limited set of `.editorconfig` properties for additional configuration. A sensible default value is provided for each property when not explicitly defined. Properties can be overridden, provided they are specified under `[*.{kt,kts}]`. Ktlint uses some properties defined by [.editorconfig](https://editorconfig.org/), IntelliJ IDEA and custom properties.
 2â†’
 3â†’!!! danger
 4â†’
 5â†’    Unfortunately [IntelliJ IDEA](https://www.jetbrains.com/idea/) has an [autoformat issue regarding `.editorconfig`](https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/IDEA-242506). Due to this error an additional space is added between glob statements, resulting in `[*{kt, kts}]` instead of `[*{kt,kts}]`. The `.editorconfig` library used by `ktlint` [ignores sections after encountering a space in the list](https://github.com/editorconfig/editorconfig/issues/148). As a result, the rule is not applied on all files as documented in the [original ktlint issue](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/762).
 6â†’
 7â†’## Code style
 8â†’
 9â†’By default, the `intellij_idea` Kotlin code style is applied. Alternatively, the code style can be set to `ktlint_official` or `android`.
10â†’
11â†’```ini
12â†’[*.{kt,kts}]
13â†’ktlint_code_style = ktlint_official
14â†’```
15â†’
16â†’!!! note
17â†’    The default code style will be changed to `ktlint_official` in the `1.0` version of ktlint.
18â†’
19â†’## Disabled rules
20â†’
21â†’!!! note
22â†’    Support of properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` has been removed in KtLint `0.49`.
23â†’
24â†’Rule sets and individual rules can be disabled / enabled with a separate property per rule (set).
25â†’
26â†’All rules in a rule set can be enabled or disabled with a rule set property. The name of the rule set property consists of the `ktlint_` prefix followed by the rule set id. Examples:
27â†’```editorconfig
28â†’ktlint_standard = disabled # Disable all rules from the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
29â†’ktlint_experimental = enabled # Enable all `experimental` rules from all rule sets provided by KtLint or other rule providers
30â†’ktlint_custom-rule-set = enabled # Enable all rules in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
31â†’```
32â†’
33â†’Rules that are marked as experimental will not be run, unless explicitly enabled:
34â†’```editorconfig
35â†’ktlint_experimental = enabled # Enable rules marked as experimental for all rule sets that are enabled
36â†’```
37â†’
38â†’An individual rule can be enabled or disabled with a rule property. The name of the rule property consists of the `ktlint_` prefix followed by the rule set id followed by a `_` and the rule id. Examples:
39â†’```editorconfig
40â†’ktlint_standard_final-newline = disabled # Disables the `final-newline` rule provided by KtLint
41â†’ktlint_standard_some-experimental-rule = enabled # Enables the (experimental) `some-experimental-rule` in the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
42â†’ktlint_custom-rule-set_custom-rule = disabled # Disables the `custom-rule` rule in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
43â†’```
44â†’
45â†’!!! note
46â†’    The *rule* properties are applied after applying the *rule set* properties and take precedence. So if a rule set is disabled but a specific rule of that rule set is enabled, then the rule will be executed.
47â†’
48â†’
49â†’## Final newline
50â†’
51â†’By default, a final newline is required at the end of the file.
52â†’
53â†’```ini
54â†’[*.{kt,kts}]
55â†’insert_final_newline = true
56â†’```
57â†’
58â†’This setting only takes effect when rule `final-newline` is enabled.
59â†’
60â†’## Force multiline function signature based on number of parameters

36â†’```
37â†’
38â†’An individual rule can be enabled or disabled with a rule property. The name of the rule property consists of the `ktlint_` prefix followed by the rule set id followed by a `_` and the rule id. Examples:
39â†’```editorconfig
40â†’ktlint_standard_final-newline = disabled # Disables the `final-newline` rule provided by KtLint
41â†’ktlint_standard_some-experimental-rule = enabled # Enables the (experimental) `some-experimental-rule` in the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
42â†’ktlint_custom-rule-set_custom-rule = disabled # Disables the `custom-rule` rule in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
43â†’```
44â†’
45â†’!!! note
46â†’    The *rule* properties are applied after applying the *rule set* properties and take precedence. So if a rule set is disabled but a specific rule of that rule set is enabled, then the rule will be executed.
47â†’
48â†’
49â†’## Final newline
50â†’
51â†’By default, a final newline is required at the end of the file.
52â†’
53â†’```ini
54â†’[*.{kt,kts}]
55â†’insert_final_newline = true
56â†’```
57â†’
58â†’This setting only takes effect when rule `final-newline` is enabled.
59â†’
60â†’## Force multiline function signature based on number of parameters

conversation_history.content:
160â†’### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
161â†’
162â†’This release is intended to be the last release before the 1.0.x release of ktlint. If all goes as planned, the 1.0.x release does not contain any new breaking changes with except of removal of functionality which is deprecated in this release.
163â†’
164â†’This release contains a lot of breaking changes which aims to improve the future maintainability of Ktlint. If you get stuck while migrating, please reach out to us by creating an issue.
165â†’
166â†’#### Experimental rules
167â†’
168â†’Rules in custom rule sets can now be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint if `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or if the rule itself has been enabled explicitly.
169â†’
170â†’When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set as that would require a distinct rule set id. When moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set this would mean that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
171â†’
172â†’#### EditorConfig
173â†’
174â†’Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. New fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
175â†’
176â†’The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
177â†’
178â†’#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
179â†’
180â†’A new code style `ktlint_official` is introduced. This code style is work in progress but will become the default code style in the `1.0` release. Please try out the new code style and provide your feedback via the [issue tracker](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues).
181â†’
182â†’This `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This code style also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in those conventions and style guide. But do note that this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor.
183â†’
184â†’The existing code styles have been renamed to make more clear what the basis of the code style is.
185â†’
186â†’The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint `0.49`. Note that the default code style will be changed to `ktlint_official` in the `1.0` release.
187â†’
188â†’Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
189â†’
190â†’#### Package restructuring and class relocation
191â†’
192â†’The internal structure of the Ktlint project has been revised. The Ktlint CLI and KtLint API modules have been decoupled where possible. Modules have been restructured and renamed. See [API Overview](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/api/overview/) for more information.
193â†’
194â†’This is the last release that contains module `ktlint-core` as it had too many responsibilities. All classes in this module are relocated to other modules. Some classes have also been renamed. See tables below for details. Classes that are left behind in the `ktlint-core` module are deprecated and were kept in this version for backwards compatibility only. The `ktlint-core` module will be removed in Ktlint `0.50.x`.
195â†’
196â†’Classes below have been moved from module `ktlint-core` to the new module `ktlint-rule-engine-core`:
197â†’
198â†’| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`                  | New class/package name in `ktlint-rule-engine-core`                               |
199â†’|----------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
200â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.editorconfig               | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig                            |
201â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigProperties     | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.EditorConfig                            |
202â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.OptInFeatures              | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.OptInFeatures                           |
203â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType                | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType                             |
204â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.package                    | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ASTNodeExtension                        |
205â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.IndentConfig                   | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig                            |
206â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Rule                           | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule                                    |
207â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleProvider                   | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleProvider                            |
208â†’
209â†’Classes below have been moved from module `ktlint-core` to the new module `ktlint-rule-engine`:
210â†’
211â†’| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`                  | New class/package name in `ktlint-rule-engine`                               |
212â†’|----------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
213â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigDefaults       | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.EditorConfigDefaults                    |
214â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigOverride       | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.EditorConfigOverride                    |
215â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.KtLintParseException       | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintParseException                    |
216â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.KtLintRuleException        | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleException                     |
217â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.KtLint                         | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleEngine                        |
218â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.LintError                      | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.LintError                               |
219â†’

conversation_history.name: read
conversation_history.content:
1â†’Ktlint uses a limited set of `.editorconfig` properties for additional configuration. A sensible default value is provided for each property when not explicitly defined. Properties can be overridden, provided they are specified under `[*.{kt,kts}]`. Ktlint uses some properties defined by [.editorconfig](https://editorconfig.org/), IntelliJ IDEA and custom properties.
 2â†’
 3â†’!!! danger
 4â†’
 5â†’    Unfortunately [IntelliJ IDEA](https://www.jetbrains.com/idea/) has an [autoformat issue regarding `.editorconfig`](https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/IDEA-242506). Due to this error an additional space is added between glob statements, resulting in `[*{kt, kts}]` instead of `[*{kt,kts}]`. The `.editorconfig` library used by `ktlint` [ignores sections after encountering a space in the list](https://github.com/editorconfig/editorconfig/issues/148). As a result, the rule is not applied on all files as documented in the [original ktlint issue](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/762).
 6â†’
 7â†’## Code style
 8â†’
 9â†’By default, the `ktlint_official` Kotlin code style is applied. Alternatively, the code style can be set to `intellij_idea` or `android_studio`.
10â†’
11â†’```ini
12â†’[*.{kt,kts}]
13â†’ktlint_code_style = ktlint_official
14â†’```
15â†’
16â†’!!! note
17â†’    The default code style will be changed to `ktlint_official` in the `1.0` version of ktlint.
18â†’
19â†’## Disabled rules
20â†’
21â†’!!! note
22â†’    Support of properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` has been removed in KtLint `0.49`.
23â†’
24â†’Rule sets and individual rules can be disabled / enabled with a separate property per rule (set).
25â†’
26â†’All rules in a rule set can be enabled or disabled with a rule set property. The name of the rule set property consists of the `ktlint_` prefix followed by the rule set id. Examples:
27â†’```editorconfig
28â†’ktlint_standard = disabled # Disable all rules from the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
29â†’ktlint_experimental = enabled # Enable all `experimental` rules from all rule sets provided by KtLint or other rule providers
30â†’ktlint_custom-rule-set = enabled # Enable all rules in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)

17â†’## Disabled rules
18â†’
19â†’!!! note
20â†’    Support of properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` has been removed in KtLint `0.49`.
21â†’
22â†’Rule sets and individual rules can be disabled / enabled with a separate property per rule (set).
23â†’
24â†’All rules in a rule set can be enabled or disabled with a rule set property. The name of the rule set property consists of the `ktlint_` prefix followed by the rule set id. Examples:
25â†’```editorconfig
26â†’ktlint_standard = disabled # Disable all rules from the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
27â†’ktlint_experimental = enabled # Enable all `experimental` rules from all rule sets provided by KtLint or other rule providers
28â†’ktlint_custom-rule-set = enabled # Enable all rules in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
29â†’```
30â†’

conversation_history.name: read
conversation_history.content:
1â†’In ktlint `0.49` the new code style `ktlint_official` is introduced. This code style is work in progress but will become the default code style in the `1.0` release. Please try out the new code style and provide your feedback via the [issue tracker](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues).
 2â†’
 3â†’```ini
 4â†’[*.{kt,kts}]
 5â†’ktlint_code_style = ktlint_official
 6â†’```
 7â†’
 8â†’This `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This code style also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in those conventions and style guide.
 9â†’
10â†’!!! note
11â†’    Be aware that this code style in some cases formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor.
12â†’
13â†’The existing code styles have been renamed to make more clear what the basis of the code style is.
14â†’
15â†’* The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with the default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `official` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`.
16â†’
17â†’* Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
18â†’

content.thinking:
**Considering code style defaults**

Iâ€™m noticing that `EditorConfig.addPropertiesWithDefaultValueIfMissing` might use computed default values based on code style. However, if the codeStyle property is missing, it has switched to using the default code style, which has changed and is causing tests to fail. That change was intentional, so I think we just need to set the default code style correctly. Additionally, we should ensure other properties and documentation reflect this shift as well. I wonder if we need to adjust CLI output too, since these changes could affect rule-specific properties.**Evaluating ktlintOfficial defaults**

The requirement indicates that the default code style has changed to ktlintOfficial defaults. While this change is intended, it could impact hidden tests that expect the previous default. I need to make sure that `ktlint_official` is ready, as it seems rules may only apply those defaults when explicitly set. Also, I wonder if this change enables experimental rules that might run by default now. That could potentially lead to issues, but if ktlint 1.0 aims for `ktlint_official` as the standard, then it makes sense to proceed with it.**Checking rule enablement**

Iâ€™m considering that the change in version 0.50 may lead to more rules running by default, affecting hidden tests. To minimize any breaking changes, it might be useful for the rules and their enablement to be independent of the code style. I need to take a look at how the rule engine uses .editorconfig for this purpose. It seems thereâ€™s an editorconfig property for each rule. I wonder if `StandardRuleSetProvider` determines the default enablement based on the code style, so I should search for `ktlint_official` there.

content.text:
Implemented the switch of the default code style from `intellij_idea` to `ktlint_official`.

### Key changes
- **Default code style**: `CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue` is now `CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official`.
- **Property description/docs**: Updated the `ktlint_code_style` property description and related docs/changelog text to reflect that `ktlint_official` is now the default.

### Files updated
- `ktlint-rule-engine-core/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/rule/engine/core/api/editorconfig/CodeStyleEditorConfigProperty.kt`
- `documentation/**/docs/rules/code-styles.md`
- `documentation/**/docs/rules/configuration-ktlint.md`
- `CHANGELOG.md`

212â†’    private fun fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(
213â†’        node: ASTNode,
214â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
215â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
216â†’        multiline: Boolean,
217â†’        dryRun: Boolean,
218â†’    ): Int {
219â†’        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
220â†’
221â†’        val primaryConstructorParameterList = node.getPrimaryConstructorParameterListOrNull()
222â†’        val hasNoValueParameters =
223â†’            primaryConstructorParameterList
224â†’                ?.children()
225â†’                .orEmpty()
226â†’                .none { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
227â†’
228â†’        whiteSpaceCorrection +=
229â†’            if (hasNoValueParameters) {
230â†’                fixWhiteSpacesInEmptyValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, dryRun)
231â†’            } else {
232â†’                fixWhiteSpacesBeforeFirstParameterInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline, dryRun) +
233â†’                    fixWhiteSpacesBetweenParametersInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline, dryRun) +
234â†’                    fixWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline, dryRun)
235â†’            }
236â†’
237â†’        return whiteSpaceCorrection
238â†’    }

240â†’    private fun fixWhiteSpacesInEmptyValueParameterList(
241â†’        node: ASTNode,
242â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
243â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
244â†’        dryRun: Boolean,
245â†’    ): Int {
246â†’        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
247â†’
248â†’        node
249â†’            .getPrimaryConstructorParameterListOrNull()
250â†’            ?.takeUnless { it.containsComment() }
251â†’            ?.let { parameterList ->
252â†’                if (!dryRun) {
253â†’                    emit(parameterList.startOffset, "No parenthesis expected", true)
254â†’                }
255â†’                if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
256â†’                    parameterList.treeParent.removeChild(parameterList)
257â†’                } else {
258â†’                    whiteSpaceCorrection -= parameterList.textLength
259â†’                }
260â†’            }
261â†’
262â†’        return whiteSpaceCorrection
263â†’    }

267â†’    private fun fixWhiteSpacesBeforeFirstParameterInValueParameterList(
268â†’        node: ASTNode,
269â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
270â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
271â†’        multiline: Boolean,
272â†’        dryRun: Boolean,
273â†’    ): Int {
274â†’        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
275â†’
276â†’        val valueParameterList = node.getPrimaryConstructorParameterListOrNull()
277â†’        val firstParameterInList =
278â†’            valueParameterList
279â†’                ?.children()
280â†’                ?.first { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
281â†’                ?: return 0
282â†’
283â†’        val firstParameter = firstParameterInList.firstChildNode
284â†’        firstParameter
285â†’            ?.prevLeaf()
286â†’            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
287â†’            .let { whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier ->
288â†’                if (multiline) {
289â†’                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier == null ||
290â†’                        !whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.textContains('\n')
291â†’                    ) {
292â†’                        if (!dryRun) {
293â†’                            emit(firstParameterInList.startOffset, "Newline expected after opening parenthesis", true)
294â†’                        }
295â†’                        // Let indent rule determine the exact indent
296â†’                        val expectedParameterIndent = indentConfig.childIndentOf(node)
297â†’                        if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
298â†’                            valueParameterList.firstChildNode.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(expectedParameterIndent)
299â†’                        } else {
300â†’                            whiteSpaceCorrection += expectedParameterIndent.length - (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier?.textLength ?: 0)
301â†’                        }
302â†’                    }
303â†’                } else {
304â†’                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier != null) {
305â†’                        if (!dryRun) {
306â†’                            emit(
307â†’                                firstParameter!!.startOffset,
308â†’                                "No whitespace expected between opening parenthesis and first parameter name",
309â†’                                true,
310â†’                            )
311â†’                        }
312â†’                        if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
313â†’                            whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.treeParent.removeChild(whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier)
314â†’                        } else {
315â†’                            whiteSpaceCorrection -= whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.textLength
316â†’                        }
317â†’                    }
318â†’                }
319â†’            }
320â†’
321â†’        return whiteSpaceCorrection
322â†’    }

324â†’    private fun fixWhiteSpacesBetweenParametersInValueParameterList(
325â†’        node: ASTNode,
326â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
327â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
328â†’        multiline: Boolean,
329â†’        dryRun: Boolean,
330â†’    ): Int {
331â†’        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
332â†’
333â†’        val valueParameterList = node.getPrimaryConstructorParameterListOrNull()
334â†’        val firstParameterInList =
335â†’            valueParameterList
336â†’                ?.children()
337â†’                ?.first { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
338â†’                ?: return 0
339â†’
340â†’        valueParameterList
341â†’            .children()
342â†’            .filter { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
343â†’            .filter { it != firstParameterInList }
344â†’            .forEach { valueParameter ->
345â†’                val firstChildNodeInValueParameter = valueParameter.firstChildNode
346â†’                firstChildNodeInValueParameter
347â†’                    ?.prevLeaf()
348â†’                    ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
349â†’                    .let { whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier ->
350â†’                        if (multiline) {
351â†’                            if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier == null ||
352â†’                                !whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.textContains('\n')
353â†’                            ) {
354â†’                                if (!dryRun) {
355â†’                                    emit(valueParameter.startOffset, "Parameter should start on a newline", true)
356â†’                                }
357â†’                                // Let IndentationRule determine the exact indent
358â†’                                val expectedParameterIndent = indentConfig.childIndentOf(node)
359â†’                                if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
360â†’                                    firstChildNodeInValueParameter.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(expectedParameterIndent)
361â†’                                } else {
362â†’                                    whiteSpaceCorrection += expectedParameterIndent.length - (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier?.textLength ?: 0)
363â†’                                }
364â†’                            }
365â†’                        } else {
366â†’                            if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier == null || whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.text != " ") {
367â†’                                if (!dryRun) {
368â†’                                    emit(firstChildNodeInValueParameter!!.startOffset, "Single whitespace expected before parameter", true)
369â†’                                }
370â†’                                if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
371â†’                                    firstChildNodeInValueParameter.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
372â†’                                } else {
373â†’                                    whiteSpaceCorrection += 1 - (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier?.textLength ?: 0)
374â†’                                }
375â†’                            }
376â†’                        }
377â†’                    }
378â†’            }
379â†’
380â†’        return whiteSpaceCorrection
381â†’    }

383â†’    private fun fixWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis(
384â†’        node: ASTNode,
385â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
386â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
387â†’        multiline: Boolean,
388â†’        dryRun: Boolean,
389â†’    ): Int {
390â†’        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
391â†’
392â†’        val closingParenthesisPrimaryConstructor =
393â†’            node
394â†’                .getPrimaryConstructorParameterListOrNull()
395â†’                ?.findChildByType(RPAR)
396â†’        closingParenthesisPrimaryConstructor
397â†’            ?.prevSibling()
398â†’            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
399â†’            .let { whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis ->
400â†’                if (multiline) {
401â†’                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis == null ||
402â†’                        !whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.textContains('\n')
403â†’                    ) {
404â†’                        if (!dryRun) {
405â†’                            emit(closingParenthesisPrimaryConstructor!!.startOffset, "Newline expected before closing parenthesis", true)
406â†’                        }
407â†’                        // Let IndentationRule determine the exact indent
408â†’                        val expectedParameterIndent = node.indent()
409â†’                        if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
410â†’                            closingParenthesisPrimaryConstructor!!.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(expectedParameterIndent)
411â†’                        } else {
412â†’                            whiteSpaceCorrection += expectedParameterIndent.length - (whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis?.textLength ?: 0)
413â†’                        }
414â†’                    }
415â†’                } else {
416â†’                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis != null &&
417â†’                        whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.nextLeaf()?.elementType == RPAR
418â†’                    ) {
419â†’                        if (!dryRun) {
420â†’                            emit(
421â†’                                whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.startOffset,
422â†’                                "No whitespace expected between last parameter and closing parenthesis",
423â†’                                true,
424â†’                            )
425â†’                        }
426â†’                        if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
427â†’                            whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.treeParent.removeChild(whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis)
428â†’                        } else {
429â†’                            whiteSpaceCorrection -= whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.textLength
430â†’                        }
431â†’                    }
432â†’                }
433â†’            }
434â†’        return whiteSpaceCorrection
435â†’    }

543â†’                                ) {
