567â†’    private fun classSignaturesIncludingFirstSuperTypeExceedsMaxLineLength(
568â†’        node: ASTNode,
569â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
570â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
571â†’    ): Boolean {
572â†’        val actualClassSignatureLength = node.getClassSignatureLength(excludeSuperTypes = false)
573â†’        // Calculate the length of the class signature in case it, including the super types, would be rewritten as single
574â†’        // line (and without a maximum line length). The white space correction will be calculated via a dry run of the
575â†’        // actual fix.
576â†’        val length =
577â†’            actualClassSignatureLength +
578â†’                // Calculate the white space correction in case the signature would be rewritten to a single line
579â†’                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = false, dryRun = true)
580â†’        return length > maxLineLength
581â†’    }

585â†’            ?.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
586â†’            ?.findChildByType(RPAR)
587â†’            ?.prevLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() }
588â†’            .isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()

594â†’    ) {
595â†’        node

597â†’            ?.let { classBody ->

600â†’                    if (autoCorrect) {

603â†’                            ?.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(" ")

622â†’    private fun List<ASTNode>.childrenBetween(
623â†’        startASTNodePredicate: (ASTNode) -> Boolean,
624â†’        endASTNodePredicate: (ASTNode) -> Boolean,
625â†’    ): List<ASTNode> {
626â†’        val iterator = iterator()
627â†’        var currentNode: ASTNode
628â†’        val childrenBetween: MutableList<ASTNode> = mutableListOf()
629â†’
630â†’        while (iterator.hasNext()) {
631â†’            currentNode = iterator.next()
632â†’            if (startASTNodePredicate(currentNode)) {
633â†’                childrenBetween.add(currentNode)
634â†’                break
635â†’            }
636â†’        }
637â†’
638â†’        while (iterator.hasNext()) {
639â†’            currentNode = iterator.next()
640â†’            childrenBetween.add(currentNode)
641â†’            if (endASTNodePredicate(currentNode)) {
642â†’                break
643â†’            }
644â†’        }
645â†’
646â†’        return childrenBetween
647â†’    }

 4â†’import mu.KotlinLogging

 6â†’
 7â†’private const val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_ANDROID_STUDIO_CODE_STYLE = 100 // https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide#line_wrapping
 8â†’private const val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_KTLINT_OFFICIAL_CODE_STYLE = 140
 9â†’private const val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF_EDITOR_CONFIG = "off"
10â†’
11â†’/**
12â†’ * Integer value that denotes that the property is to be considered as disabled.
13â†’ */
14â†’public const val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF: Int = Int.MAX_VALUE
15â†’
16â†’private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
17â†’private var isInvalidValueLoggedBefore = false
18â†’
19â†’public val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<Int> =
20â†’    EditorConfigProperty(
21â†’        name = PropertyType.max_line_length.name,
22â†’        type = PropertyType.max_line_length,
23â†’        defaultValue = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF,
24â†’        androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_ANDROID_STUDIO_CODE_STYLE,
25â†’        intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF,
26â†’        ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_KTLINT_OFFICIAL_CODE_STYLE,
27â†’        propertyMapper = { property, codeStyleValue ->
28â†’            when {
29â†’                property == null || property.isUnset -> {
30â†’                    codeStyleValue.defaultValue()
31â†’                }
32â†’
33â†’                /**
34â†’                 * Internally, Ktlint uses integer 'Int.MAX_VALUE' to indicate that the max line length has to be ignored as this is easier
35â†’                 * in comparisons to check whether the maximum length of a line is exceeded.
36â†’                 */
37â†’                property.sourceValue == MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF_EDITOR_CONFIG -> MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF
38â†’
39â†’                else ->
40â†’                    PropertyType
41â†’                        .max_line_length
42â†’                        .parse(property.sourceValue)
43â†’                        .let {
44â†’                            if (!it.isValid) {
45â†’                                if (!isInvalidValueLoggedBefore) {
46â†’                                    isInvalidValueLoggedBefore = true
47â†’                                    LOGGER.warn { "Found invalid '.editorconfig' property value: ${it.errorMessage}" }
48â†’                                }
49â†’                                if (it.source == "-1") {
50â†’                                    MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF
51â†’                                } else {
52â†’                                    codeStyleValue.defaultValue()
53â†’                                }
54â†’                            } else {
55â†’                                it.parsed
56â†’                            }
57â†’                        }
58â†’            }
59â†’        },
60â†’        propertyWriter = { property ->
61â†’            if (property <= 0 || property == MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF) {
62â†’                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF_EDITOR_CONFIG
63â†’            } else {
64â†’                property.toString()
65â†’            }
66â†’        },
67â†’    )
68â†’
69â†’private fun CodeStyleValue.defaultValue() =
70â†’    when (this) {
71â†’        CodeStyleValue.android_studio -> MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_ANDROID_STUDIO_CODE_STYLE
72â†’        CodeStyleValue.intellij_idea -> MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF
73â†’        CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official -> MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_KTLINT_OFFICIAL_CODE_STYLE
74â†’    }

100â†’            CodeStyleValue.intellij_idea -> intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue
101â†’            CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official -> ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue
102â†’        }
103â†’
104â†’    /**
105â†’     * Checks whether a property with name [propertyName] is defined.
106â†’     */

108â†’
109â†’    /**
110â†’     * Gets the parsed value of the property with type [PropertyType]. Returns null if the property type is not found. Can only be used for
111â†’     * properties for which the name of the property is identical to the name of the type.

113â†’    private fun <T> EditorConfigProperty<T>.getParsedValueOrDefault(): T? =

115â†’
116â†’    /**

118â†’     * found. Intended to be used for properties for which the name is not identical to the name of the property type.
119â†’     */
120â†’    private fun <T> PropertyType<T>.getParsedValueOrDefault(

122â†’        defaultValue: T,
123â†’    ): T =
124â†’        getPropertyValue(propertyName)
125â†’            .let { propertyValue ->
126â†’                if (propertyValue.isValid) {
127â†’                    propertyValue.parsed
128â†’                } else {
129â†’                    LOGGER.warn {
130â†’                        "Editorconfig property '$propertyName' contains an invalid value '${propertyValue.source}'. The default value " +
131â†’                            "'$defaultValue' is used instead."
132â†’                    }
133â†’                    defaultValue
134â†’                }
135â†’            }

138â†’        parse(
139â†’            properties[propertyName]?.sourceValue,
140â†’        )

142â†’    /**

144â†’     */
145â†’    public fun <T> map(mapper: (Property) -> T): Collection<T> =

22â†’     * (default is `[*.{kt,kts}]`) content including expected default values.
23â†’     *
24â†’     * @return Kotlin section editorconfig content. For example:
25â†’     * ```properties

CHANGELOG.md:9:* Add rule `class-signature`. This rule rewrites the class header to a consistent format. In code style `ktlint_official`, super types are always wrapped to a separate line. In other code styles, super types are only wrapped in classes having multiple super types. Especially for code style `ktlint_official` the class headers are rewritten in a more consistent format. See [examples in documentation](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/latest/rules/experimental/#class-signature). `class-signature` [#875](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1349), [#1349](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/875)
--
CHANGELOG.md:17:* Do not indent class body for classes having a long super type list in code style `ktlint_official` as it is inconsistent compared to other class bodies `indent` [#2115](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2115)
CHANGELOG.md:18:* In code style `ktlint_official` do not indent the closing parenthesis of a PARENTHESIZED expression `indent` [#920](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/920)
--
CHANGELOG.md:67:* Add new experimental rule `no-empty-file` for all code styles. A kotlin (script) file may not be empty ([#1074](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1074))
--
CHANGELOG.md:108:* Allow to 'unset' the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_function_signature_rule_force_multiline_when_parameter_count_greater_or_equal_than` when using `ktlint_official` code style `function-signature` ([#1977](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1977))
--
CHANGELOG.md:111:* Restrict indentation of closing quotes to `ktlint_official` code style to keep formatting of other code styles consistent with `0.48.x` and before `indent` ([#1971](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1971))
CHANGELOG.md:112:* Extract rule `no-single-line-block-comment` from `comment-wrapping` rule. The `no-single-line-block-comment` rule is added as experimental rule to the `ktlint_official` code style, but it can be enabled explicitly for the other code styles as well. ([#1980](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1980))
--
CHANGELOG.md:174:Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. New fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
--
CHANGELOG.md:178:#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
--
CHANGELOG.md:180:A new code style `ktlint_official` is introduced. This code style is work in progress but will become the default code style in the `1.0` release. Please try out the new code style and provide your feedback via the [issue tracker](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues).
--
CHANGELOG.md:182:This `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This code style also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in those conventions and style guide. But do note that this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor.
--
CHANGELOG.md:184:The existing code styles have been renamed to make more clear what the basis of the code style is.
--
CHANGELOG.md:186:The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint `0.49`. Note that the default code style will be changed to `ktlint_official` in the `1.0` release.
--
CHANGELOG.md:188:Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
--
CHANGELOG.md:381:* Add new code style `ktlint_offical`. The code style is work in progress and should be considered a preview. It is intended to become the default code style in the next release. Please try it out and give your feedback. See [code styles](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/code-styles/) for more information. The following rules have been added to the `ktlint_official` code style (the rules can also be run for other code styles when enabled explicitly):
--
CHANGELOG.md:385:  * Add new experimental rule `try-catch-finally-spacing`. This rule enforces consistent spacing in try-catch, try-finally and try-catch-finally statement. This rule can also be run for other code styles, but then it needs to be enabled explicitly.
--
CHANGELOG.md:403:* Add new experimental rule `enum-wrapping` for all code styles. An enum should either be a single line, or each enum entry should be defined on a separate line. ([#1903](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1903))
--
CHANGELOG.md:416:* The parameters of a function literal containing a multiline parameter list are aligned with first parameter whenever the first parameter is on the same line as the start of that function literal (not allowed in `ktlint_official` code style) `indent` ([#1756](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1756))
--
CHANGELOG.md:418:* Fix continuation indent for a dot qualified array access expression in `ktlint_official` code style only `indent` ([#1540](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1540))
--
CHANGELOG.md:436:* Wrap annotated function parameters to a separate line in code style `ktlint_official` only. `function-signature`, `parameter-list-wrapping` ([#1908](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1908))
--
CHANGELOG.md:439:* Wrap annotations before class constructor in code style `ktlint_official`. `annotation` ([#1916](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1916))
--
CHANGELOG.md:444:* Wrap the parameters of a function literal containing a multiline parameter list (only in `ktlint_official` code style) `parameter-list-wrapping` ([#1681](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1681)).
--
CHANGELOG.md:453:* For the new code style `ktlint_official`, do not allow wildcard imports `java.util` and `kotlinx.android.synthetic` by default. Important: `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand` needs to be set to value `unset` in order to enforce IntelliJ IDEA default formatter to not generate wildcard imports `no-wildcard-imports` ([#1797](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1797))
--
CHANGELOG.md:932:This property defaults to the `official` Kotlin code style when not set.

1â†’Ktlint uses a limited set of `.editorconfig` properties for additional configuration. A sensible default value is provided for each property when not explicitly defined. Properties can be overridden, provided they are specified under `[*.{kt,kts}]`. Ktlint uses some properties defined by [.editorconfig](https://editorconfig.org/), IntelliJ IDEA and custom properties.
 2â†’
 3â†’!!! danger
 4â†’
 5â†’    Unfortunately [IntelliJ IDEA](https://www.jetbrains.com/idea/) has an [autoformat issue regarding `.editorconfig`](https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/IDEA-242506). Due to this error an additional space is added between glob statements, resulting in `[*{kt, kts}]` instead of `[*{kt,kts}]`. The `.editorconfig` library used by `ktlint` [ignores sections after encountering a space in the list](https://github.com/editorconfig/editorconfig/issues/148). As a result, the rule is not applied on all files as documented in the [original ktlint issue](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/762).
 6â†’
 7â†’## Code style
 8â†’
 9â†’By default, the `intellij_idea` Kotlin code style is applied. Alternatively, the code style can be set to `ktlint_official` or `android`.
10â†’
11â†’```ini
12â†’[*.{kt,kts}]
13â†’ktlint_code_style = ktlint_official
14â†’```
15â†’
16â†’!!! note
17â†’    The default code style will be changed to `ktlint_official` in the `1.0` version of ktlint.
18â†’
19â†’## Disabled rules
20â†’
21â†’!!! note
22â†’    Support of properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` has been removed in KtLint `0.49`.
23â†’
24â†’Rule sets and individual rules can be disabled / enabled with a separate property per rule (set).
25â†’
26â†’All rules in a rule set can be enabled or disabled with a rule set property. The name of the rule set property consists of the `ktlint_` prefix followed by the rule set id. Examples:
27â†’```editorconfig
28â†’ktlint_standard = disabled # Disable all rules from the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
29â†’ktlint_experimental = enabled # Enable all `experimental` rules from all rule sets provided by KtLint or other rule providers
30â†’ktlint_custom-rule-set = enabled # Enable all rules in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
31â†’```
32â†’
33â†’Rules that are marked as experimental will not be run, unless explicitly enabled:
34â†’```editorconfig
35â†’ktlint_experimental = enabled # Enable rules marked as experimental for all rule sets that are enabled
36â†’```
37â†’
38â†’An individual rule can be enabled or disabled with a rule property. The name of the rule property consists of the `ktlint_` prefix followed by the rule set id followed by a `_` and the rule id. Examples:
39â†’```editorconfig
40â†’ktlint_standard_final-newline = disabled # Disables the `final-newline` rule provided by KtLint
41â†’ktlint_standard_some-experimental-rule = enabled # Enables the (experimental) `some-experimental-rule` in the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
42â†’ktlint_custom-rule-set_custom-rule = disabled # Disables the `custom-rule` rule in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
43â†’```
44â†’
45â†’!!! note
46â†’    The *rule* properties are applied after applying the *rule set* properties and take precedence. So if a rule set is disabled but a specific rule of that rule set is enabled, then the rule will be executed.
47â†’
48â†’
49â†’## Final newline
50â†’
51â†’By default, a final newline is required at the end of the file.
52â†’
53â†’```ini
54â†’[*.{kt,kts}]
55â†’insert_final_newline = true
56â†’```
57â†’
58â†’This setting only takes effect when rule `final-newline` is enabled.
59â†’
60â†’## Force multiline function signature based on number of parameters

1â†’In ktlint `0.49` the new code style `ktlint_official` is introduced. This code style is work in progress but will become the default code style in the `1.0` release. Please try out the new code style and provide your feedback via the [issue tracker](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues).
 2â†’
 3â†’```ini
 4â†’[*.{kt,kts}]
 5â†’ktlint_code_style = ktlint_official
 6â†’```
 7â†’
 8â†’This `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This code style also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in those conventions and style guide.
 9â†’
10â†’!!! note
11â†’    Be aware that this code style in some cases formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor.
12â†’
13â†’The existing code styles have been renamed to make more clear what the basis of the code style is.
14â†’
15â†’* The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `official` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint `0.49`. Be aware that the default code style will be changed to `ktlint_official` in the `1.0` release.
16â†’
17â†’* Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
18â†’

1â†’Ktlint uses a limited set of `.editorconfig` properties for additional configuration. A sensible default value is provided for each property when not explicitly defined. Properties can be overridden, provided they are specified under `[*.{kt,kts}]`. Ktlint uses some properties defined by [.editorconfig](https://editorconfig.org/), IntelliJ IDEA and custom properties.
 2â†’
 3â†’!!! danger
 4â†’
 5â†’    Unfortunately [IntelliJ IDEA](https://www.jetbrains.com/idea/) has an [autoformat issue regarding `.editorconfig`](https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/IDEA-242506). Due to this error an additional space is added between glob statements, resulting in `[*{kt, kts}]` instead of `[*{kt,kts}]`. The `.editorconfig` library used by `ktlint` [ignores sections after encountering a space in the list](https://github.com/editorconfig/editorconfig/issues/148). As a result, the rule is not applied on all files as documented in the [original ktlint issue](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/762).
 6â†’
 7â†’## Code style
 8â†’
 9â†’By default, the `intellij_idea` Kotlin code style is applied. Alternatively, the code style can be set to `ktlint_official` or `android`.
10â†’
11â†’```ini
12â†’[*.{kt,kts}]
13â†’ktlint_code_style = ktlint_official
14â†’```
15â†’
16â†’!!! note
17â†’    The default code style will be changed to `ktlint_official` in the `1.0` version of ktlint.
18â†’
19â†’## Disabled rules
20â†’
21â†’!!! note
22â†’    Support of properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` has been removed in KtLint `0.49`.
23â†’
24â†’Rule sets and individual rules can be disabled / enabled with a separate property per rule (set).
25â†’
26â†’All rules in a rule set can be enabled or disabled with a rule set property. The name of the rule set property consists of the `ktlint_` prefix followed by the rule set id. Examples:
27â†’```editorconfig
28â†’ktlint_standard = disabled # Disable all rules from the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
29â†’ktlint_experimental = enabled # Enable all `experimental` rules from all rule sets provided by KtLint or other rule providers
30â†’ktlint_custom-rule-set = enabled # Enable all rules in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
31â†’```
32â†’
33â†’Rules that are marked as experimental will not be run, unless explicitly enabled:
34â†’```editorconfig
35â†’ktlint_experimental = enabled # Enable rules marked as experimental for all rule sets that are enabled
36â†’```
37â†’
38â†’An individual rule can be enabled or disabled with a rule property. The name of the rule property consists of the `ktlint_` prefix followed by the rule set id followed by a `_` and the rule id. Examples:
39â†’```editorconfig
40â†’ktlint_standard_final-newline = disabled # Disables the `final-newline` rule provided by KtLint
41â†’ktlint_standard_some-experimental-rule = enabled # Enables the (experimental) `some-experimental-rule` in the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
42â†’ktlint_custom-rule-set_custom-rule = disabled # Disables the `custom-rule` rule in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
43â†’```
44â†’
45â†’!!! note
46â†’    The *rule* properties are applied after applying the *rule set* properties and take precedence. So if a rule set is disabled but a specific rule of that rule set is enabled, then the rule will be executed.
47â†’
48â†’
49â†’## Final newline
50â†’
51â†’By default, a final newline is required at the end of the file.
52â†’
53â†’```ini
54â†’[*.{kt,kts}]
55â†’insert_final_newline = true
56â†’```
57â†’
58â†’This setting only takes effect when rule `final-newline` is enabled.
59â†’
60â†’## Force multiline function signature based on number of parameters

160â†’### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
161â†’
162â†’This release is intended to be the last release before the 1.0.x release of ktlint. If all goes as planned, the 1.0.x release does not contain any new breaking changes with except of removal of functionality which is deprecated in this release.
163â†’
164â†’This release contains a lot of breaking changes which aims to improve the future maintainability of Ktlint. If you get stuck while migrating, please reach out to us by creating an issue.
165â†’
166â†’#### Experimental rules
167â†’
168â†’Rules in custom rule sets can now be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint if `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or if the rule itself has been enabled explicitly.
169â†’
170â†’When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set as that would require a distinct rule set id. When moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set this would mean that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
171â†’
172â†’#### EditorConfig
173â†’
174â†’Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. New fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
175â†’
176â†’The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
177â†’
178â†’#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
179â†’
180â†’A new code style `ktlint_official` is introduced. This code style is work in progress but will become the default code style in the `1.0` release. Please try out the new code style and provide your feedback via the [issue tracker](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues).
181â†’
182â†’This `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This code style also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in those conventions and style guide. But do note that this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor.
183â†’
184â†’The existing code styles have been renamed to make more clear what the basis of the code style is.
185â†’
186â†’The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint `0.49`. Note that the default code style will be changed to `ktlint_official` in the `1.0` release.
187â†’
188â†’Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
189â†’
190â†’#### Package restructuring and class relocation
191â†’
192â†’The internal structure of the Ktlint project has been revised. The Ktlint CLI and KtLint API modules have been decoupled where possible. Modules have been restructured and renamed. See [API Overview](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/api/overview/) for more information.
193â†’
194â†’This is the last release that contains module `ktlint-core` as it had too many responsibilities. All classes in this module are relocated to other modules. Some classes have also been renamed. See tables below for details. Classes that are left behind in the `ktlint-core` module are deprecated and were kept in this version for backwards compatibility only. The `ktlint-core` module will be removed in Ktlint `0.50.x`.
195â†’
196â†’Classes below have been moved from module `ktlint-core` to the new module `ktlint-rule-engine-core`:
197â†’
198â†’| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`                  | New class/package name in `ktlint-rule-engine-core`                               |
199â†’|----------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
200â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.editorconfig               | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig                            |
201â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigProperties     | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.EditorConfig                            |
202â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.OptInFeatures              | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.OptInFeatures                           |
203â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType                | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType                             |
204â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.package                    | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ASTNodeExtension                        |
205â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.IndentConfig                   | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig                            |
206â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Rule                           | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule                                    |
207â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleProvider                   | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleProvider                            |
208â†’
209â†’Classes below have been moved from module `ktlint-core` to the new module `ktlint-rule-engine`:
210â†’
211â†’| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`                  | New class/package name in `ktlint-rule-engine`                               |
212â†’|----------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
213â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigDefaults       | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.EditorConfigDefaults                    |
214â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigOverride       | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.EditorConfigOverride                    |
215â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.KtLintParseException       | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintParseException                    |
216â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.KtLintRuleException        | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleException                     |
217â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.KtLint                         | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleEngine                        |
218â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.LintError                      | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.LintError                               |
219â†’

1â†’In ktlint `0.49` the new code style `ktlint_official` was introduced. Starting with ktlint `1.0`, this is the default code style. Please provide feedback via the [issue tracker](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues).
 2â†’
 3â†’```ini
 4â†’[*.{kt,kts}]
 5â†’ktlint_code_style = ktlint_official
 6â†’```
 7â†’
 8â†’This `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This code style also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in those conventions and style guide.
 9â†’
10â†’!!! note
11â†’    Be aware that this code style in some cases formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor.
12â†’
13â†’The existing code styles have been renamed to make more clear what the basis of the code style is.
14â†’
15â†’* The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `official` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint `0.49`. Be aware that the default code style will be changed to `ktlint_official` in the `1.0` release.
16â†’
17â†’* Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
18â†’

1â†’Ktlint uses a limited set of `.editorconfig` properties for additional configuration. A sensible default value is provided for each property when not explicitly defined. Properties can be overridden, provided they are specified under `[*.{kt,kts}]`. Ktlint uses some properties defined by [.editorconfig](https://editorconfig.org/), IntelliJ IDEA and custom properties.
 2â†’
 3â†’!!! danger
 4â†’
 5â†’    Unfortunately [IntelliJ IDEA](https://www.jetbrains.com/idea/) has an [autoformat issue regarding `.editorconfig`](https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/IDEA-242506). Due to this error an additional space is added between glob statements, resulting in `[*{kt, kts}]` instead of `[*{kt,kts}]`. The `.editorconfig` library used by `ktlint` [ignores sections after encountering a space in the list](https://github.com/editorconfig/editorconfig/issues/148). As a result, the rule is not applied on all files as documented in the [original ktlint issue](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/762).
 6â†’
 7â†’## Code style
 8â†’
 9â†’By default, the `intellij_idea` Kotlin code style is applied. Alternatively, the code style can be set to `ktlint_official` or `android`.
10â†’
11â†’```ini
12â†’[*.{kt,kts}]
13â†’ktlint_code_style = ktlint_official
14â†’```
15â†’
16â†’
17â†’## Disabled rules
18â†’
19â†’!!! note
20â†’    Support of properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` has been removed in KtLint `0.49`.
21â†’
22â†’Rule sets and individual rules can be disabled / enabled with a separate property per rule (set).
23â†’
24â†’All rules in a rule set can be enabled or disabled with a rule set property. The name of the rule set property consists of the `ktlint_` prefix followed by the rule set id. Examples:
25â†’```editorconfig
26â†’ktlint_standard = disabled # Disable all rules from the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
27â†’ktlint_experimental = enabled # Enable all `experimental` rules from all rule sets provided by KtLint or other rule providers
28â†’ktlint_custom-rule-set = enabled # Enable all rules in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
29â†’```
30â†’
